Structured educational format with a youth-oriented article pattern, presenting weather facts using boldened terms and a friendly tone ("The Weather Channel").

The phrase "Clouds are often visible, sometimes roaring loudly or completely invisible — even hidden" suggests a well-known poetic description of clouds, completing a list of cloud qualities ("always present but sometimes hidden, or entirely obscured").

Final token "hidden" ends mid-phrase ("either completely visible or sometimes hidden... either entirely visible or completely hidden"), part of an enumerated list describing cloud's nature ("even the sun, sometimes masked..."), strongly expecting "under the clouds" or "from view" or "within the atmosphere," completing the metaphor.
</explanation>

Final token "view view" ends mid-sentence ("hidden from sight, and obscured by clouds, often a mystery that can disappear and hidden from view"), strongly expecting "." or "in the atmosphere" or "it" to close the metaphor, completing the example of a hidden natural phenomenon.
